mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth over the trailing three months positively drives S&P 500 forward 20-day returns because rising output signals demand strength and earnings momentum, supporting equity valuations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains at or below zero over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not correlate positively with trailing 3-month IP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail and food services sales growth over the prior month indicates robust consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns immediately after release.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 5-day forward windows falls below 0 for any consecutive 1-year period, or negative average forward return in top decile of RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate hike above the effective lower bound signals the start of a tightening cycle, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: After trigger, forward 60-day SPX average return is not negative or t-test vs. non-triggered periods fails at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1700 thousand units, it signals sustained demand in the construction sector and broad economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, average 20-day SPX return is non-positive, or hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rate and accelerating CPI inflation above 2% annualized generates negative real interest rates, which may squeeze profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5 and 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 2.0
failure_condition: After trigger, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of cases or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (monthly change >200K) and a decline in the unemployment rate (>0.1pp) reinforce the labor market recovery narrative, boosting investor confidence and producing positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200 and UNRATE monthly change < -0.1
failure_condition: In triggered months, average forward 5-day SPX return is not positive, or fewer than 60% of instances show positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of ultra-low interest rates (federal funds rate at or below 0.25 percent), equity markets rally due to low discount rates and portfolio rebalancing toward risky assets, generating positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns regardless of other macro signals.
failure_condition: During ZIRP periods, average 60-day SPX return is negative, or the strategy has negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: In the early recovery regime (industrial production below its pre-recession peak but rising), further gains in industrial production signal that the supply side is normalizing, supporting sustained economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
regime_hint: early recovery (INDPRO below 110 but rising from trough)
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds 110, the mechanism fails or forward returns become negative; or within the regime, average 20-day SPX return is not positive over any 1-year window.
